reasoning: The Lean statement captures each element of the informal claim: an arbitrary group G, an arbitrary index type I (no countability/finiteness constraint), the nonempty collection hypothesis via `[Nonempty I]`, each member of the family being normal via `hH`, and the conclusion that the intersection `⨅ (i : I), H i` (the infimum in `Subgroup G`, which equals the set-theoretic intersection) is normal.
